---
title: "Test 02: Enrichment Analysis"
output: html_document
---

## Goal

This test focuses on the GSEA workflow in the project.

Students should practice:

- reading GSEA result tables
- extracting important columns
- comparing different GSEA outputs
- explaining what a GSEA result means in simple language

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
cur <- tryCatch(knitr::current_input(), error = function(e) NULL)
path <- if (is.null(cur)) NULL else if (is.character(cur)) cur else cur[["path"]]
if (!is.null(path) && length(path) > 0) {
  project_root <- normalizePath(file.path(dirname(path[1]), ".."), mustWork = FALSE)
} else {
  wd <- getwd()
  project_root <- if (dir.exists(file.path(wd, "Data"))) wd else normalizePath(file.path(wd, ".."), mustWork = FALSE)
}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = TRUE, warning = TRUE)
library(dplyr)
gsea_go <- read.csv(file.path(project_root, "Results", "04_Enrichment", "GSEA_GO.csv"))
gsea_kegg <- read.csv(file.path(project_root, "Results", "04_Enrichment", "GSEA_KEGG.csv"))
hallmark <- read.csv(file.path(project_root, "Results", "04_Enrichment", "Hallmark_GSEA.csv"))
```

## Question 1

Inspect the three GSEA result tables.

Tasks:

- print `dim(gsea_go)`
- print `dim(gsea_kegg)`
- print `dim(hallmark)`
- print the first 3 descriptions from `gsea_go`

```{r q1}
# Guidance code:
# dim(gsea_go)
# dim(gsea_kegg)
# dim(hallmark)
# head(gsea_go$Description, 3)

# TODO
```

## Question 2

Extract key columns from GSEA GO and GSEA KEGG results.

Tasks:

- from `gsea_go`, keep `Description`, `NES`, `p.adjust`, and `setSize`
- from `gsea_kegg`, sort by `p.adjust` from small to large
- print the first 5 pathway descriptions from the sorted GSEA KEGG table

```{r q2}
# Guidance code:
# gsea_go_small <- gsea_go %>% select(Description, NES, p.adjust, setSize)
# head(gsea_go_small, 5)
# gsea_kegg_sorted <- gsea_kegg %>% arrange(p.adjust)
# head(gsea_kegg_sorted$Description, 5)

# TODO
```

## Question 3

Use Hallmark GSEA results to compare positive and negative enrichment.

Tasks:

- create `hallmark_positive` using `NES > 0`
- create `hallmark_negative` using `NES < 0`
- print the first 5 descriptions from each table

```{r q3}
# Guidance code:
# hallmark_positive <- hallmark %>% filter(NES > 0)
# hallmark_negative <- hallmark %>% filter(NES < 0)
# head(hallmark_positive$Description, 5)
# head(hallmark_negative$Description, 5)

# TODO
```

## Short Answer

In 2 to 3 sentences, explain:

- what a positive `NES` usually suggests
- why GSEA results are often sorted by adjusted p value
